title: Giorgia Meloni becomes prime minister of Italy
fact: Giorgia Meloni is an Italian politician who has served as Prime Minister of Italy since October 2022.

title: Silvio Berlusconi becomes prime minister of Italy
fact: Silvio Berlusconi was an Italian media tycoon and politician who served as the prime minister of Italy in three governments from 1994 to 1995, 2001 to 2006 and 2008 to 2011.

title: Giuseppe Conte becomes prime minister of Italy
fact: Giuseppe Conte is an Italian jurist, academic, and politician who served as prime minister of Italy from June 2018 to February 2021.

title: Aldo Moro becomes prime minister of Italy
fact: Aldo Moro was an Italian statesman and prominent member of Christian Democracy (DC) and its centre-left wing.

title: Mario Draghi becomes prime minister of Italy
fact: Mario Draghi is an Italian politician, economist, academic, banker, statesman, and civil servant, who served as the prime minister of Italy from 13 February 2021 to 22 October 2022.

title: Matteo Renzi becomes prime minister of Italy
fact: Matteo Renzi is an Italian politician who served as prime minister of Italy from 2014 to 2016.

title: Romano Prodi becomes prime minister of Italy
fact: Romano Prodi is an Italian politician who served as President of the European Commission from 1999 to 2004 and twice as Prime Minister of Italy, from 1996 to 1998, and again from 2006 to 2008.

title: Giulio Andreotti becomes prime minister of Italy
fact: Giulio Andreotti was an Italian politician and statesman who served as the 41st prime minister of Italy in seven governments.

title: Alcide De Gasperi becomes prime minister of Italy
fact: Alcide Amedeo Francesco De Gasperi was an Italian politician and statesman who founded the Christian Democracy party and served as prime minister of Italy in eight successive coalition governments from 1945 to 1953.

title: Bettino Craxi becomes prime minister of Italy
fact: Benedetto "Bettino" Craxi was an Italian politician and statesman, leader of the Italian Socialist Party (PSI) from 1976 to 1993, and the 45th prime minister of Italy from 1983 to 1987.

title: Enrico Letta becomes prime minister of Italy
fact: Enrico Letta is an Italian politician who served as Prime Minister of Italy from April 2013 to February 2014, leading a grand coalition of centre-left and centre-right parties.

title: Carlo Azeglio Ciampi becomes prime minister of Italy
fact: Carlo Azeglio Ciampi was an Italian politician, statesman and banker who was the president of Italy from 1999 to 2006 and prime minister of Italy from 1993 to 1994.

title: Paolo Gentiloni becomes prime minister of Italy
fact: Paolo Gentiloni Silveri is an Italian politician who was European Commissioner for Economy in the von der Leyen Commission from 1 December 2019 to 30 November 2024.

title: Francesco Cossiga becomes prime minister of Italy
fact: Francesco Maurizio Cossiga was an Italian politician who served as the president of Italy from 1985 to 1992.

title: Mario Monti becomes prime minister of Italy
fact: Mario Monti is an Italian politician, economist and academic who served as the prime minister of Italy from 2011 to 2013, leading a technocratic government in the wake of the Italian debt crisis.

title: Amintore Fanfani becomes prime minister of Italy
fact: Amintore Fanfani was an Italian politician and statesman who served as 32nd prime minister of Italy for five separate terms.

title: Giovanni Leone becomes prime minister of Italy
fact: Giovanni Leone was an Italian politician, jurist and university professor who was the president of Italy from 1971 to 1978.

title: Giuliano Amato becomes prime minister of Italy
fact: Giuliano Amato is an Italian politician who twice served as Prime Minister of Italy, first from 1992 to 1993 and again from 2000 to 2001.

title: Antonio Segni becomes prime minister of Italy
fact: Antonio Segni was an Italian politician and statesman who served as the president of Italy from 1962 to 1964, and as Prime Minister of Italy from 1955 to 1957 and again from 1959 to 1960.

title: Massimo D'Alema becomes prime minister of Italy
fact: Massimo D'Alema is an Italian politician and journalist who was the 53rd prime minister of Italy from 1998 to 2000.

title: Lamberto Dini becomes prime minister of Italy
fact: Lamberto Dini is an Italian politician and economist.

title: Ciriaco De Mita becomes prime minister of Italy
fact: Luigi Ciriaco De Mita was an Italian politician and statesman who served as Prime Minister of Italy from April 1988 to July 1989.

title: Arnaldo Forlani becomes prime minister of Italy
fact: Arnaldo Forlani was an Italian politician who served as Prime Minister of Italy from 1980 to 1981.

title: Giovanni Goria becomes prime minister of Italy
fact: Giovanni Giuseppe Goria was an Italian politician.

title: Giovanni Spadolini becomes prime minister of Italy
fact: Giovanni Spadolini was an Italian politician and statesman, who served as the 44th prime minister of Italy.

title: Mariano Rumor becomes prime minister of Italy
fact: Mariano Rumor was an Italian politician and statesman.
